Author: Pavan Vamsi
Title: Your AI coding sessions are invisible. So I made them searchable.
Text:
Member-only story
Your AI coding sessions are invisible. So I made them searchable.
Copilot Lens indexes your Copilot CLI, VS Code Copilot Chat, and Claude Code sessions, with full-text search, across all three, entirely on your machine.
Three weeks ago I spent 45 minutes with GitHub Copilot working through a gnarly connection pooling issue. We found a clean solution. I shipped it, closed the terminal, and moved on.
Last week a colleague hit the exact same problem.
I had nothing. The session was gone… not deleted, just invisible. Sitting in a directory somewhere as raw JSONL, completely unsearchable.
That’s the problem Copilot Lens solves.
What is Copilot Lens?
It’s a local web dashboard that runs on your machine and makes your AI coding history searchable and browsable. No cloud. No sign-in. No data leaving your laptop. That’s it.
npx copilot-lens --open
It reads directly from the directories these tools already write to.
